- **Develop**: Study fundamentals of Develop.

**Soft Skills & Values:** Missing indicators for collaborative workflows or specific values (e.g., 'Innovation', 'Mentorship') highlighted in the JD. Consider highlighting team-based achievements.

jobs.title: Data Analyst
jobs.description:
Job Title: Data Analyst
Job Overview

We are seeking a detail-oriented and analytical Data Analyst to collect, process, and perform statistical analyses on large datasets. The ideal candidate will translate data into actionable insights that support business decisions and improve performance.

Key Responsibilities
Collect, clean, and validate data from multiple sources
Analyze datasets to identify trends, patterns, and anomalies
Develop dashboards, reports, and visualizations (e.g., using Power BI, Tableau)
Collaborate with stakeholders to understand business needs and deliver insights
Perform statistical analysis and interpret results
Maintain databases and ensure data integrity
Automate data processes and reporting where possible
Present findings in a clear and concise manner to non-technical audiences
Required Skills & Qualifications
Bachelor’s degree in Data Science, Statistics, Mathematics, Computer Science, or related field
Proficiency in SQL and at least one programming language (e.g., Python or R)
Experience with data visualization tools (Tableau, Power BI, or similar)
Strong analytical and problem-solving skills
Knowledge of Excel (advanced functions, pivot tables)
Understanding of data modeling and database design
Excellent communication and presentation skills
Preferred Qualifications
Experience with big data tools (e.g., Hadoop, Spark)
Familiarity with machine learning concepts
Experience in a specific industry (finance, healthcare, e-commerce, etc.)
Knowledge of cloud platforms (AWS, Azure, or Google Cloud)
Key Competencies
Attention to detail
Critical thinking
Time management
Business acumen
Ability to work independently and in teams
Typical Tools & Technologies
SQL
Python / R
Excel
Tableau / Power BI
Databases (MySQL, PostgreSQL, etc.)
Work Environment

Data Analysts typically work in office or remote environments, collaborating with cross-functional teams such as marketing, finance, and IT.

jobs.title: AI/ML Engineer
jobs.description:
Key Responsibilities
1. Model Development & Design
Design, build, and train sophisticated Machine Learning (ML) and Deep Learning (DL) models (e.g., CNNs, RNNs, Transformers, GANs).
Implement and optimize ML algorithms and tools to meet business objectives.
Transform data science prototypes into production-ready software systems. 
Accenture
Accenture
 +5
2. Data Engineering & Preprocessing
Select appropriate datasets, perform data collection, and transform raw data into usable formats.
Construct and maintain scalable data pipelines for training and testing.
Perform feature engineering to enhance model performance. 
nexus IT group
nexus IT group
 +4
3. Deployment & MLOps
Deploy ML models to production (cloud or edge platforms) using Docker, Kubernetes, or serverless architectures.
Implement MLOps best practices to manage the full model lifecycle, including version control, automated retraining, and monitoring for drift.
Optimize models for performance, scalability, and efficiency in real-time or batch environments. 
Accenture
Accenture
 +4
4. Research & Collaboration
Stay updated on the latest advancements in AI/ML (e.g., Generative AI, LLMs) and suggest improvements to existing systems.
Collaborate with data scientists, software developers, and product managers to integrate AI solutions into larger applications. 
nexus IT group
nexus IT group
 +4
Required Skills & Qualifications
Education: Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in Computer Science, AI, Machine Learning, or a related field.
Experience: 2–5+ years of professional experience in AI/ML model development and deployment.
Programming Languages: Expert-level proficiency in Python (including NumPy, Pandas, Scikit-learn). Experience with Java or R is often advantageous.
ML Frameworks: Strong hands-on experience with TensorFlow, PyTorch, or Keras.
Tools & Platforms: Experience with Cloud platforms (AWS SageMaker, GCP Vertex AI, or Azure ML) and Docker.
Knowledge Base: Strong foundational knowledge in mathematics, statistics, algorithms, and data structures.
Specialized Areas: Experience with NLP, Computer Vision, or Generative AI/LLMs is highly desirable. 
Accenture
Accenture
 +9
Commonly Used Tech Stack
Languages: Python, SQL, Java
Libraries: Scikit-learn, XGBoost, PyTorch, TensorFlow, Pandas, NumPy
MLOps: MLflow, Docker, Kubernetes, Databricks, Weights & Biases
Cloud: AWS (S3, SageMaker), GCP, Azure 
Accenture
Accenture
 +4
Soft Skills
Analytical Problem-Solving: Ability to solve complex, non-standardized problems.
Communication: Ability to explain technical concepts to non-technical stakeholders.
Adaptability: Passion for continuous learning in a fast-changing field. 
Accenture
Accenture
 +5
Typical Compensation (US Based on 2026 Projections) 
Entry Level (0-2 years): $70k – $95k+.
Mid-Level (3-5 years): $95k – $140k+.
Senior/Staff Level: $140k – $200k+.
Note: Salaries vary widely based on location, company size, and specific AI sub-fields. 
Vettio
Vettio
 +1

Communication: Ability to explain technical concepts to non-technical stakeholders.
Adaptability: Passion for continuous learning in a fast-changing field.

jobs.title: AI/ML Engineer
jobs.description:
Key Responsibilities
1. Model Development & Design
Design, build, and train sophisticated Machine Learning (ML) and Deep Learning (DL) models (e.g., CNNs, RNNs, Transformers, GANs).
Implement and optimize ML algorithms and tools to meet business objectives.
Transform data science prototypes into production-ready software systems. 
Accenture
Accenture
 +5
2. Data Engineering & Preprocessing
Select appropriate datasets, perform data collection, and transform raw data into usable formats.
Construct and maintain scalable data pipelines for training and testing.
Perform feature engineering to enhance model performance. 
nexus IT group
nexus IT group
 +4
3. Deployment & MLOps
Deploy ML models to production (cloud or edge platforms) using Docker, Kubernetes, or serverless architectures.
Implement MLOps best practices to manage the full model lifecycle, including version control, automated retraining, and monitoring for drift.
Optimize models for performance, scalability, and efficiency in real-time or batch environments. 
Accenture
Accenture
 +4
4. Research & Collaboration
Stay updated on the latest advancements in AI/ML (e.g., Generative AI, LLMs) and suggest improvements to existing systems.
Collaborate with data scientists, software developers, and product managers to integrate AI solutions into larger applications. 
nexus IT group
nexus IT group
 +4
Required Skills & Qualifications
Education: Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in Computer Science, AI, Machine Learning, or a related field.
Experience: 2–5+ years of professional experience in AI/ML model development and deployment.
Programming Languages: Expert-level proficiency in Python (including NumPy, Pandas, Scikit-learn). Experience with Java or R is often advantageous.
ML Frameworks: Strong hands-on experience with TensorFlow, PyTorch, or Keras.
Tools & Platforms: Experience with Cloud platforms (AWS SageMaker, GCP Vertex AI, or Azure ML) and Docker.
Knowledge Base: Strong foundational knowledge in mathematics, statistics, algorithms, and data structures.
Specialized Areas: Experience with NLP, Computer Vision, or Generative AI/LLMs is highly desirable. 
Accenture
Accenture
 +9
Commonly Used Tech Stack
Languages: Python, SQL, Java
Libraries: Scikit-learn, XGBoost, PyTorch, TensorFlow, Pandas, NumPy
MLOps: MLflow, Docker, Kubernetes, Databricks, Weights & Biases
Cloud: AWS (S3, SageMaker), GCP, Azure

 +4
Soft Skills
Analytical Problem-Solving: Ability to solve complex, non-standardized problems.
Communication: Ability to explain technical concepts to non-technical stakeholders.
Adaptability: Passion for continuous learning in a fast-changing field.

Typical Compensation (US Based on 2026 Projections) 
Entry Level (0-2 years): $70k – $95k+.
Mid-Level (3-5 years): $95k – $140k+.
Senior/Staff Level: $140k – $200k+.
Note: Salaries vary widely based on location, company size, and specific AI sub-fields.
